Adiantum is a genus of plants commonly known as maidenhair ferns, a type of vascular plant characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds. The health benefits of Adiantum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Adiantum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adiantum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
